name: List
description: Lists present a vertical run of related records — drivers, notifications, recent vehicles. Each row pairs a primary line with optional supporting text, a leading avatar/icon, and trailing actions. Built from @mui/material/List as the <ul> container, composed with ListItem/ListItemButton/ListItemText/ListItemIcon/ListItemAvatar children — List has no `items` prop.
keyRule: Use a list for simple, scannable records. The moment you need columns, sorting, selection, or pagination, switch to a DataGrid.

variants.name: Single-line
variants.whenToUse: Short records (names, options). ListItemText with only `primary` set.

variants.name: Two-line
variants.whenToUse: A title plus one supporting detail (most common). ListItemText with `primary` + `secondary`.

variants.name: Three-line
variants.whenToUse: Title plus two lines of context — secondary content that wraps, or ListItemText's secondaryTypographyProps for multi-line text.

variants.name: With controls
variants.whenToUse: Leading Checkbox (multi-select via ListItemIcon) or trailing Switch/action.

anatomy.name: List container (this component)
anatomy.description: holds the items; may be divided (Divider) or grouped (ListSubheader).

anatomy.name: ListItem / ListItemButton
anatomy.description: separate @mui/material components — one record. Use ListItemButton for a clickable/navigable row (it, not ListItem, carries hover/focus/selected states).

anatomy.name: ListItemIcon / ListItemAvatar
anatomy.description: separate @mui/material components — leading icon, checkbox, or avatar.

anatomy.name: ListItemText
anatomy.description: a separate @mui/material component rendering the primary and secondary text.

anatomy.name: ListItemSecondaryAction
anatomy.description: a separate @mui/material component/pattern for a trailing meta value, action, or overflow ⋮.

anatomy.name: ListSubheader / Divider
anatomy.description: separate @mui/material components for an optional group label or separator.

behaviors.name: Activation
behaviors.description: Use ListItemButton (not a plain ListItem) for a clickable row that navigates or opens detail — it provides the real hover/focus/selected treatment.

behaviors.name: Selection
behaviors.description: For multi-select, use a leading Checkbox inside ListItemIcon on each ListItemButton (and consider whether a DataGrid is the better tool).

behaviors.name: Grouping
behaviors.description: Use ListSubheader + Divider to group long lists; avoid deep nesting.

behaviors.name: Dense mode
behaviors.description: Set `dense` for compact vertical padding suited to keyboard/mouse-heavy UIs; it propagates to descendant list items via context.

states.treatment: 4% overlay (MUI's real action.hover opacity) on ListItemButton.

specs.value: Not an enforced token — MUI's ListItem/ListItemButton size to their content and padding (~8px vertical padding by default, less with `dense`); 48/64/88px are typical single/two/three-line outcomes, not fixed values
Inherits the app-wide default Typography variant (body2, 14px/400) unless ListItemText's primaryTypographyProps overrides it
16px (ListItem's real default padding); 16-ish gap to a leading ListItemIcon/ListItemAvatar via MUI's own spacing, not a semantic.spacing token directly

props.name: children
props.description: ListItem/ListItemButton (and ListSubheader/Divider) elements — there is no `items` array prop; render the records yourself.

props.name: dense
props.description: Compact vertical padding for the list and its descendant items (propagated via context). Default false.

props.name: disablePadding
props.description: Removes the list's vertical padding. Default false.

props.name: subheader
props.description: Normally a ListSubheader element, rendered above the items.

props.name: component
props.description: Overrides the rendered root element/component. Default 'ul'.

props.name: sx
props.description: Standard MUI system prop for one-off style overrides.

doThis: Use ListItemText's primary + secondary for record + context.
Switch to a DataGrid when columns/sorting/pagination appear.
dontDoThis: Don't cram tabular data into a list.
Don't rely on a plain ListItem for a clickable row — use ListItemButton so focus/hover/selected states are correct.
accessibility: List renders a real <ul> by default (real list semantics).
Use ListItemButton (a real button-like element) for interactive rows so they have accessible names and visible focus automatically.
Leading checkboxes should be wired to their item's ListItemText via aria-labelledby or by wrapping both in the same ListItemButton's label.

related.name: Menu
related.why: Action lists inside a temporary overlay, not a persistent list

related.name: Drawer
related.why: A List is the typical children of a navigation-style Drawer
